steps.title: Check current dragonfly deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dragonfly version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dragonfly -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly
helm list -n dragonfly 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dragonfly installation.

steps.title: Review dragonfly configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dragonfly configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dragonfly -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly
kubectl get configmap -n dragonfly -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dragonfly
```
Missing PFCOUNT command on mastodon installation

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Replace redis to dragonfly on mastodon installation
2. Load the mastodon web
3. Web empty
4.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Missing PFCOUNT
steps.description:
This version does not create sparse-encoded HLLs, however it is fully compatible with such ones created by Redis as it converts them to the dense encoding.
```yaml
[83618058-3981-4ac1-9d89-7d913e276f63] method=HEAD path=/health format=*/* controller=HealthController action=show status=200 duration=0.36 view=0.19 db=0.00
[8d0133b7-154c-445d-8392-caa4ff3d7cf0] method=GET path=/api/v1/instance format=*/* controller=Api::V1::InstancesController action=show status=200 duration=1.51 view=0.28 db=0.00
[f40159fd-6247-4f67-9f03-4e5de65b2a11] method=GET path=/.well-known/nodeinfo format=json controller=WellKnown::NodeInfoController action=index status=200 duration=1.02 view=0.51 db=0.00
[86aedd5c-da14-4bcd-9df2-899cfd971401] method=GET path=/nodeinfo/2.0 format=*/
```

steps.title: Upgrade dragonfly to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade dragonfly:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dragonfly
helm list -n dragonfly
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dragonfly -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly
kubectl get events -n dragonfly --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Missing PFCOUNT" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This version does not create sparse-encoded HLLs, however it is fully compatible with such ones created by Redis as it converts them to the dense encoding.
codeSnippets:
[83618058-3981-4ac1-9d89-7d913e276f63] method=HEAD path=/health format=*/* controller=HealthController action=show status=200 duration=0.36 view=0.19 db=0.00
[8d0133b7-154c-445d-8392-caa4ff3d7cf0] method=GET path=/api/v1/instance format=*/* controller=Api::V1::InstancesController action=show status=200 duration=1.51 view=0.28 db=0.00
[f40159fd-6247-4f67-9f03-4e5de65b2a11] method=GET path=/.well-known/nodeinfo format=json controller=WellKnown::NodeInfoController action=index status=200 duration=1.02 view=0.51 db=0.00
[86aedd5c-da14-4bcd-9df2-899cfd971401] method=GET path=/nodeinfo/2.0 format=*/* controller=WellKnown::NodeInfoController action=show status=500 error='Redis::CommandError: ERR unknown command `PFCOUNT`' duration=1.96 view=0.00 db=0.00
[86aedd5c-da14-4bcd-9df2-899cfd971401]   
[

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dragonfly installed or the issue environment reproducible.
